task_language: Pick the akita black bowl next to the cookies box and place it on the plate
strategy_notes: MULTI-ATTEMPT EXPLORATION solve. Scene localization (agentview hi-res + world_hi_00.npy, robust to wrist refinement): cookies_box at world (+0.076, +0.020, z=0.92); plate at (-0.265, -0.140, z=0.938) — partially occluded behind black cabinet at back-left, confirmed by SAM3 score=0.555 and wrist refine score=0.973 to (-0.256, -0.152, z=0.94); ramekin (red-rimmed) at (-0.215, +0.198, z=0.91) — NOT a target; two akita_black_bowls: LBowl (image-front-left) at world (+0.078, -0.169, z=0.955) and RBowl (image-front-right) at (+0.040, +0.157, z=0.95). Distance-to-cookies analysis: RBowl ~0.14m, LBowl ~0.19m — RBowl appeared 'closer/next to' under cosine intuition. KEY FINDING (overrides distance heuristic): the predicate is bound to akita_black_bowl_1 (NAMED-OBJECT predicate). Attempt-A picked RBowl, placed bowl perfectly on plate center (visually seated in plate well, gripper retracted, bowl world at (-0.305, -0.153)) — predicate REMAINED FALSE. After reset, Attempt-B picked LBowl with pi0_pick max_chunks=15 (chunks=22 first failed with non-descent), measured bowl-eef offset (varied during carry: initial (+0.047, -0.056) at lift, then (+0.003, +0.033) after waypoint+over-plate move), placed eef at (-0.259, -0.185, z=1.01) — predicate FIRED. So LBowl = akita_black_bowl_1, even though it is the more distant of the two from cookies. The 'next to' language in the SWAP variant is misleading — the predicate identity is fixed; pick by NAMED bowl, not by closest-to-relation. Lessons for future swap-variant cells with duplicate-class targets: (a) the goal predicate binds to a specific named instance _1; (b) closest-by-distance is unreliable when positions are swapped; (c) try both candidates across resets — placement-on-correct-surface but predicate-false is the signature of WRONG-OBJECT, not wrong-physics; (d) pi0_pick from a low (z=1.05) pre-position with max_chunks<=15 grasps reliably while z=1.10 + chunks=22 can fail to descend; (e) bowl-eef offset drifts during long carries — re-measure with wrist world map (filter pixels within ~10cm xy of eef and 1.05<z<1.16) before final descent. Path from front-bowl-area to back-left-plate clears the cabinet by waypointing via (-0.10, -0.05, z=1.18) then to (-0.30, ~y, 1.10) before final descent.

pick_result.name: pick
pick_result.instruction: pick up the akita black bowl
pick_result.target_bowl: LBowl == akita_black_bowl_1 (front-right of robot frame, image-left)
